text:
Once upon a time in a small forest, lived an old owl named Olive. She had feathers the color of autumn leaves and an eye that twinkled like starlight. Despite her wisdom, Olive never grew old.

Olive had a pet raven named Rufus. Rufus was unique; he could talk! Mostly, he just chattered about the weather.

One crisp autumn morning, as the forest hummed with the songs of birds returning, Rufus fluttered down from his perch and perched on Olive's broad shoulder.

"Owlhoot," Rufus chirped, "I've got a tale for you!"

Olive leaned back. "Go on."

It turned out that several rabbits had vanished each night. Rufus wasn't sure how they went missing; he couldn't hear any nocturnal chats.

"Why don't you listen?" Olive joked. "You've got beautiful voices."

Embarrassed but delighted, Rufus began a series of chirps detailing his nighttime patrols.
